title: hemionitis lutea
intro: Hemionitis lutea, also known as golden polypody, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hemionitis lutea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or teas, and can also be applied topically as an ointment or compress to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
